top_profane.text:
Did I read the synopsis before jumping into Dick by Scott Hildreth?

Nope.

So you had no idea what Dick was going to be about?

Correct!

Do you regret your choice of reading Dick?

HECK FREAKING NO!

I know I shouldn’t continue to say “yes” to reading books, whether purchased by me or given for review, if I haven’t done the necessary research to ensure that I’ll even like the book, since it could be disastrous for both the author and me. In the case of Dick by Scott Hildreth the gamble I took really paid off, so I’m extremely happy this worked out for me. Since I’ve only read Ex-Con it wasn’t guaranteed that Dick would be something I would like, but now that I’ve finished, I skipped right over like and fell right in that forever type of love with Dick.

My first thought while reading, and when I finished, Dick was, “if the Joker and Harley Quinn were even a little bit sane and had a Robin Hood complex, Dick would definitely be their story.” Reading Dick was like watching a long awaited movie, or an episode from one of my favorite TV shows since the reading of it was like an action packed, steamy, and completely unhinged live action story in my head. Instead of reading like a story was being told, I felt like I was a fly on a wall into the lives of Dick and Jess, and it was freaking surreal – in a great way.
top_profane.title: If the Joker and Harley Quinn Were a Little Bit Sane and Had a Robin Hood Complex

- 'Make Me Yours' b/w 'Dearest Lee' (Joy catalog 45-231)
- 'This Is Goodbye' b/w 'Don't Pity Me' (Almont catalog number 312)

By the late 1960s he was a member of the Rhode Island based horn outfit The Blue Jays who recorded a 1970 LP "Nascence" for Map City. That seems to have somehow led to a solo deal with Map City, resulting in the release of 1970's cleverly-titled "Dick Domane". Working with producer Pete Anders and most of The Blue Jays, the album showcased a largely original set of material with a heavy emphasis on late-1960s-styled pop. top_profane.text:
Geoffrey Sanborn achieves a great deal in this compact book, not the least of which is providing for many readers a fresh basis for engaging with Melville's 'Moby-Dick'.

Sanborn, an English professor at Amherst College, launches his effort with advice he provides his students, "Moby-Dick wasn't meant, I tell them, to be a rigorous, depleting experience, a triathlon for the homebound. It was meant to be a stimulant to thought and feeling, it was meant to make your mind a more interesting and enjoyable place" (p. 2).

Authors such as Hannah Arendt and Toni Morrison are cited and effectively used to open the veins of Melville's work to discovery. Other Melville works are productively mined, as well.

In the process, Melville is transformed from the author of a long, rambling, impious tale holding a deep secret known only to the cognoscenti into a daring novelist who succeeds marvelously at portraying life full of its physical and human complexity. (fn1) Indeed, just as a person learns and adapts from life experiences, so the devotee of 'Moby-Dick' is urged to learn and adapt to re-readings of the masterpiece.

In his book 'Quixote: The Novel and the World', Ilan Stavans provides this definition of a classic, "... a book that is a mirror in which we see ourselves reflected; a book that accumulates interpretations; a book in which we meet
top_profane.title: The Marvel of Melville Reimagined

This story starts off with Gilson, a US government official, coming to an isolated, remote military base to clean up a mess that has been made. He arrives at the site and sees a house. But. Apparently, it is a window to another time (think you got it figured out, right?). Note: I would like to thank author Rebecca Morris, NetGalley, and WildBlue Press for a free ARC in exchange for an honest review of this book. I'm an avid reader of the true crime genre and I'm always looking forward to getting my hands on another book!

Richard "Dick" Kitchel was a 17-year-old high school student who never returned from a party in Corvallis, Oregon in 1967. His body was found 10 days later floating in the Willamette River. It was clear that he had been strangled and that some sort of struggle occurred before his body was dumped in the river (the strangulation killed him and there was no water in his lungs, ruling out drowning). The condition of the body, having been exposed to the river's elements and fish, was not helpful in determining much about the murder. Similarly, since it had been 10 days and no one really knew Dick had been murdered (most young teens have willingly left home, according to the detectives in the 1960s) any potential crime scenes also lacked physical evidence.

Detectives relied heavily on testimonies of individuals that had seen Dick that night. They began to piece together information, listened to various theories about what could have happened to Dick, but unfortunately... the case went cold.

Author Rebecca Morris gives a voice to Dick, opening up the case and discussing what she recalled at the time of
top_profane.title: More of a memoir than true-crime, but interesting nonetheless!

The mother, Winifred Banks (Glynis Johns, whose film career began in 1938), is preoccupied with demonstrating as a suffragette and maintaining a clean home.  Their young children, Jane (Karen Dotrice) and Michael (Matthew Garber, who died at the young age of 21 in 1977), are allowed minimal time with their parents, who prefer to have a nanny look after and raise their children for them.  Unfortunately, Jane and Michael rarely like any of their nannies, who regularly quit after the children do something mischievous to each of them. 4 stars. A great, if a tad general/folksy, PTO tanker memoir and a great memoir portraying the overall military experience in the Pacific.

Robert Dick was a tank driver in the 763rd Tank Battalion during World War 2. He saw action on Leyte in 1944 and later on Okinawa in 1945 after an extensive experience in the US Army.

There is a lot on Dick's pre-combat experience in the infantry before he joins an armored unit and ships out to the Pacific. Several anecdotes commonly seen in memoirs from soldiers who were in the army before/just around the attack on Pearl Harbor (poorly organized into bizarre guard duties). Dick finds himself in charge of checkpoints at important spots on the West Coast and details several charming stories of soldier antics on this topic.

Eventually Dick joins the 763rd Tank Battalion and becomes a driver of an American Sherman tank (tank number 60 and in, IIRC, C Company, leading to the Cutthroats nickname coinage). He is extensive in describing his combat role and the functions and tech of the tanks and this is presented in an interesting way. Here Dick does a great job of describing the mechanics and other technical aspects of armored warfare that is easi
top_profane.title: A thoroughly enjoyable account of tank warfare in the Pacific

As much as I like Crushed, I feel that Vagabond was deeper—in both theme and plot. It was pretty cool that Vagabond explores issues of racism, and of racial and biracial identities. Gosh, yes, that “Where are you really from?” question is terrible. I am frankly baffled why so many people don’t know how offensive this question is to POC (people of color) individuals. It’s much rarer for a white person to be asked about their “country of origin” or “ethnic roots”! Anyway, I found this aspect of Vagabond very interesting, especially as I’m Chinese myself. Race is a sensitive but also necessary discussion topic.

Nevertheless, I’m very glad that Ludlow does know about Asians, despite living all his life (if I remember right) in a small town. I once read about a character who lived all her life in a small town in England, and had consequently never seen Asians before! Which really shocked me.

Aside from the topic of race, I also loved the other themes in Vagabond. One theme was on being a perpetual outsider, where you’re always the odd one out, never fitting in anywhere. I feel like a misfit in most settings too, for example, because I’m bi and nonbinary trans, as well as being on the aro ace spectra. It can get pretty lonely, even if you have supportive friends, and classmates and teachers who respect you. So, I would say I can

Ramsey did a fantastic job with Banshee and it fits perfectly with Royal Harlots which is the sister club to the Royal Bastards. If you are familiar with the Royal Bastards then you will definitely enjoy Banshee. If you are not familiar with the Royal Bastards then I suggest you read at least Savage Heat from K.L. Ramsey which is the first book from K.L. Ramsey in the Royal Bastards MC series. Savage Heat was in the Royal Bastards MC first run. Royal Bastards MC is a collection of MC stories from some of the best writers of MC romances. I highly recommend Royal Harlots, Banshee (Royal Harlots), Royal Bastards MC series, and K.L. Ramsey as an author. You won’t regret it. Happy Reading 📖!

Pushing the Limit is one of the MC story that is included. Pushing the Limit is about a strong heroine named Peppermint and how she came to belong to the Royal Harlots and fell in love with Dash who is a club member of the Royal Bastards. The Royal Bastards and the Royal Harlots support and help each other. I e

top_profane.text:
Boy, oh, boy! If I knew cowboys are that hot, I would have started reading western romance a lot sooner! And Cora Seton's cowboys are nothing if not scorching, deliciously hot! Since I had the extreme luck of winning all three of the first books from her Chance Creek series, I enjoyed some cowboy lovin' at its best!

"I’m just a man, Autumn. A flawed man. But I love you more than you will ever know." - Ethan Cruz

First we have Ethan and Autumn's story(yeah, I know, I cringed at her name, too).

Ethan Cruz is screwed every way from here to next Thursday. His parents died in an accident, leaving him to pay their debts, his sister turned her back on him when he asked her for help, and the woman he thought he loved dumped him to find greener pastures. To make matters worse, one of his best friends recorded him while - in his drunken state - he listed what a cowboy's wife should be like and uploaded it on YouTube, making it look like an ad for an email order bride. Now Ethan must choose between sending Autumn Leeds, who came to Chance Creek, Montana, to be that bride, back home to New York, or keeping up the charade and see if he can have his HEA with a woman that's nothing like the materialistic females he's had in his life so far. But Autumn has a secret of her own. She's not really here to stay, but to write a juicy, humiliating article on E
top_profane.title: Find me a cowboy, and fast!

top_profane.text:
This book was quite a fun read. A joyful romp with a chubby journalist girl and her hot, nearly bi-sexual girlfriend. Chubby is out for the scoop of a lifetime. Vampires are killing prostitutes in L.A., and this frisky Scooby-Doo duo are the only ones who can solve the case!

This book reminds me a lot of Janet Evanovich's One for the Money, in it's tone and pace. The witty banter, and crazy girl antics are fun to read. But, it's a bit shallow.

I mean, of course it's shallow. It's not really meant to be anything else, is it? It's just written to be a fun book. No extra thinking required, right?

Unlike Janet Evanovich's books, the characters in this book are even more empty. I mean, why is Chubby such a bitch? She's not written like she's had a rough life. Nobody really treats her like a fat girl. Guys even flirt with her. So, why's she such a bitch?

Her friend Tatum is a slut, I get that. The world needs sluts, and they don't need to be any deeper than they are. Sluts are cardboard characters. But, it would have been a more interesting story if she wasn't really a slut. Like, maybe she had a reason for what she was doing. Maybe she had daddy issues, who the hell knows. But, dagnabbit, in a book as long as this one is, 300 some pages, I really want more depth from a story.

And this story doesn't even merit 300 pages. It's a s
top_profane.title: The Scene - is full of bimbos and bubblegum

top_profane.text:
"Visibly Broken" , absolutely, it nearly broke me, be warned this story is on the dark side.
With warnings aside I still give this one a solid three and a half stars. Jason Stanley, son of the mayor has grown up with all sorts of opportunities , but oppurtunities mean nothing when your abusive father uses you as his fighting bag. Now a grown man and living with his girlfreind Missy Jason is determined to break the cycle of abuse. Jason" Cobra" Stanley has turned to MMA fighting to help release his steam and help to keep the abuse out of his home life , which for the most part he succeeds . Most part being the operative words,because his girlfriend Missy well , she just is trouble on two legs, Missy is aware of Jason's short fuse and his desire to never touch her I anger but when she does not get her way she purposely pushes his buttons , leading to a scene or two were Jason's upbringing comes in to play. On one such night Jason storms out to a underground fight that lands him in the hospital, badly beaten, unconscious and alone . He awakes to a beautiful blonde angel, Lorraine Bosch sitting at his bedside , but when they start pushing for information , his name, address and insurance he beats a hasty retreat, pain of no pain and also makes the decision that after three years he and Missy are over, they are just to toxic for one another to spite loving her .
top_profane.title: Little Dark, very broken!

top_profane.text: Crappy writing, crappy storyline, crappy grammar, crappy dialogue, crappy sex scenes, crappy characters, crappy ending, crappy everything. I don't remember the last time I read such crap.
top_profane.title: Absolute Crap

top_rant.text:
It’s me! Back again for more punishment! Actually, people seem to be thinking my thorough reviews are helpful, so who am I to stop now?

Before I get into the book itself, I first have to say the titles of these books are like George RR Martin’s book title’s silly cousins. In GRRM’s books, you get an idea of what’s going on. “A Game of Thrones”: sounds like politics, and it was. “A Clash of Kings”: sounds like fighting, and it was. “A Dance With Dragons”: … ok, so he dropped the ball on that one, but the other four were pretty spot on, I think. For these books, however… well, maybe “shade” is the multiple noun of vampire? You know, like a murder of crows? A shade of vampires? (Although I think the multiple most people use is “kiss”, which was made popular in the Anita Blake books.) The second one… uhm… I guess it could be referring to something being colored in blood? Maybe? The third one, now, uuuuhmmm.... Although a sand castle was referred to in the books before as being something “beautiful but temporary”, so I think it’s a throwback, but really… if you asked someone what a book called “A Castle of Sand” was about they wouldn’t even dream to answer vampires. Wouldn’t even cross their minds.

The second thing I want to point out is that in the book description, it says “A sinister secret lies in wait for Sophia in the bowls of Egypt….” The bowls. Really.
top_rant.title: DANGER, WILL ROBINSON, DANGER!

JAX~#1
OMG, where do I begin....First of I wasn't sure I was going to really like this book to much. I have over 10 years experience with kids in car-seats! In the last 6 years or so I've had nearly 50 kids buckled through my Britax Regent and Frontier 85 car-seats and have been very happy with them overall especially the Regent! I have to say that overall I am honestly disappointed in the new Frontier Click-Tight 90. The "Click-Tight" system is truly one main awesome things going for this seat, that and better shoulder pads, but beyond that I do not like the seat at all and here's why:

1) "The Headrest" Is Pure Garbage! This probably bothers me more than any other part of the seat! IT Sits Too High! IT's extremely thin and flimsy and yet they have the nerve to put a "Side Impact Protection" label on it! It's extremely thin plastic with a piece of foam on it! That's IT! This is advanced technology? BS!! I could snap the entire headrest off with my hand if I wanted to without even trying and this is suppose to stop a side impact?? Really?? It rattles when driving the car with no child in the seat which is extremely annoying and just screams cheesy junk and reminds me each time of how much money I spent on this seat and how much I hate it. IN contrast the Frontier 85 headrest was a lot more solid and sturdy and the kids also found it a lot more comfortable too! Even so the Britax Regent was the most solid car-seat I ever owned! I
top_rant.title: BRITAX FRONTIER Click-Tight (90)  Click-Tight is Awesome  The rest of the seat, Not So Much!

When I first heard about this anthology I knew I had to have it. I mean sci-fi and sexy-times! Not just yes but hell yes! I knew I would like it just by the list of authors on that beautiful cover. I already adored and respect so many of them. But I didn’t just like this anthology, I loved it!
Each story was unique, original, well written. I was glued to the pages until I finished it. It only took me a few evening to finish this bad boy too! And that is saying a lot, ‘cause this baby is one huge book! 574 pages! Just sayin’!

After reading this anthology I now have even more authors added to my drop everything and read list. And I need 10 of these babies to continue! Please! Don’t make a girl beg. Screw that, I will beg to know what happens next in a few of these.

Dirty Bad Wrong Review:

Wow, that was one hell of a whirlwind of dirty, bad and wrong stuff, all wrapped up in an additively good package.

Well the blurb alone had me wanting this book huge amounts. I absolutely love boundary pushing content, and stories, and get stupid excited when another one is talked about.
This book seemed to rock my newsfeed when it first came out, but as per usual I couldn't read it, as I had so many others to read first. But finally I managed to get started, and I literally could not put it down!!

Lydia Marsh is at the end of an epically bad relationship, and she had reached her limit when awfully boring, and dull boyfriend, decides to get drunk and nasty with Barbie from work. Then thinking he has got her pregnant, spills all to Lydia. Lydia wisely bolts, and fast.

While at work, she realises how crap her life is, and is spotted having a breakdown in the kitchen, by gorgeous, dark and totally mysterious hottie from management. James.

This sparks off an epic battle of wills from the pair of them. It's obvious they are stupidly attracted to each other, but Lydia is post break up, and James is, well he is completely wrong for her, and does not get involved with work colleagues. Ever.
So this all sounds like a regular story right? Wrong, dirty, bad, wrong.

So, Lydia needs a place t
top_rant.title: A Dirty, Bad Box set of perfection! 5 Huge filthy stars!

top_rant.text:
I figure I'll make a note about each story as I read them, so far as a whole the book is definitely worthy of 5 stars!

Sole by Alessandra Torres- This can be read by itself, but it's about the couple from Hollywood Dirt which I haven't read yet. But reading this story bout a birthday party they have for one of their children only served to make me want their WHOLE story and to know how they got their HEA in the first place!

Begging for More by Kim Karr- This is a story about a couple from Toxic, but they weren't the main characters in that story. James and Lindsay go on a last minute, spur of the moment trip to Vegas after basically just meeting each other. Things get hot....and heavy in a very serious way.

Heartbreak by Skye Warren- Okay...I'm not sure how I feel about this one. It's great writing...don't get me wrong, but I was so pissed off after reading it that I couldn't think clearly. Basically it's about a girl named Hannah who makes the decision to tell a HUGE LIE about a guy named Blue that has the potential to ruin the rest of his life. She did this after he saved her from something horrible! She has her excuses as to why she did it but none of them make sense to me, they just made me mad. Their story continues in Better When it Hurts and I'm definitely going to read it because I'm really hoping that Hannah suffers deeply from
top_rant.title: Full of sexy, great reads!!  Best anthology I've ever read!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Cash Gardner is a sexy bastard that is sexy with a capital S. Okay, maybe sexy is not a strong enough word to describe this man that will make your heart stop with a kiss and panties unless. Seriously, after a few seconds with this devastatingly sexy bastard, you will need an ice cold shower to cool off.

Cash is the resident man-whore, a love 'em and leave 'em kind of guy. He's got a secret that he's been keeping for years and sees no reason to share it.

Savannah Sunday is looking for love in all the wrong places, but there is a reason for her wrong choices. Her past is something that she's not ready to talk about.

Savy and Cash have been flirting but their friends have been telling them to not cross that line, it won't end well. But you know how to goes when everyone tells you no, you just need one little taste.

And once that line is crossed, just one taste has our couple can't keeping their hands to themselves. But you know secrets don't stay secret forever. Can our couple overcome the lies and make a future or will they walk away from each other.

This book is the highly anticipated second book from Eve Jagger and it is well worth the wait. It is everything you expect from Eve and then some. This story is provocative for sure, but it also proves that sometimes the best things comes from those chances you take. From start

top_five_star_obscene.text:
* I received an ebook in exchange for an honest review. *

There are 3 things that I know I am going to get when I start reading a book by author, Scott Hildreth...in no particular order they are 1. Chucks 2. Hot Sex and 3. Curse words...and I love all 3 of those things! Author Scott Hildreth takes the reader into a whole new universe with his stories and they are one that I will gladly continue to go into! I love his sense of humor, his characters, his quirks but most of all I love the plots that he adds to each book. He continues to write amazing books that leave the reader satisfied and fulfilled...and the story is good too!!

There are plenty of (hot) sex scenes, lot's of laughs, but most importantly there are lessons to be learned and characters to fall in love with. Dick and Jess are no exception and perhaps they are one of my favorite couples so far...Dick is well, a Dick and Jess is pretty much his match in all ways that matter. She doesn't take his sh*t, she enjoys their adventures and she isn't afraid of going to jail for her man. Dick can't believe that he has possibly found the one and that she is so perfect for him....the plot is fast moving and leaves the reader wanting more while at the same time wanting to see how the story ends. The story flows so smoothly that the reader doesn't know when to stop for a break, so they just keep going
top_five_star_obscene.title: Another 5 Star read complete with chucks, swearing and love~

top_five_star_obscene.text: Dirty Lion Bastard by Renee George is a great book. I enjoyed the storyline and the characters. Dirty Lion Bastard is part of the More Than Mated Kindle World. Dirty Lion Bastard is about Scottie Monroe and Shane Largos. Scottie and her family own the Pitch & Paw Bar and Motel which is a safe haven for shifters passing through the territories. Scotties one rule is life is no hooking up with the customers. Shane had traveled to Georgia to find and challenge his father for all of the pain and heartaches that he caused his mother before she died. However, things get more complicated when he meets Scottie for the first time and realize that she is his mate. Now he has to reevaluate his plans. Dirty Lion Bastard sucked me in and kept me entertained until the end of the book. Thank you, Renee George, for another great book. I received this free book in exchange for an honest review.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Loved It!!!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I fell in love with both characters. Dick was an absolute dick and I loved him for it!
Jess is a fun smart mouth girl I loved right from the beginning! Together these two were crazy, sexy, adventurous and oh so much fun to read about.

Jess finds herself attracted to the demanding confident, cocky Dick who is every bit of his namesake. But there is something about him that makes her want to know him better so she agree to have dinner. One night that’s what they agreed to! That one night she becomes entangled in a world of car chases, diamond thief's and drug dealers. Add her witty smart mouth to his dick-ish charm and swagga I found myself eating up every word of the story! The chemistry between them are off the charts and full of drama!

Scott’s book are always an auto-read for me so I never read the description, blurbs or anything before diving into is books. WELL!!! What an adventure this story took me on! This has definitely left me with a Scott Hildreth hangover.
top_five_star_obscene.title: An amazing fun read!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Any Day That Ends In YA - Savage Delight by Sara Wolf

What is more beautiful than a sunset over the ocean or the early blooms of summer? Well some people might say the smile of their child and others might say the ruins of Angkor Wat... for me it's the defeat of Jack Hunter. To watch the Ice Man fall is a truly a stunning thing and Sara Wolf, I give you two thumbs up and then I will find you more thumbs because you deserve a sea of them. It tickles my tickles that Savage Delight was the delightful inner feelies of a thawing Ice Prince.
BUT it wasn't a Jackfest... (when I say that it sounds kind of creepy pornographic) I will toss in the Isis schtuff that was just as good as before. She is wickedly witty, awesomely snarky, fantastically snappy, greatly warmly loyal to friends and when with Jack she is like a cat rubbed backwards. The amnesia schtuff that was frustrating, crustating and plain old turn-me-into-a-werewolf and make me howl at the moon maddening. I love her. I want her to have what I have--which is the book, so she knows what the hell's bells is going on! Why won't you just give her a YouTube tutorial on girl engines and boy engines and how to grease motors? Le sigh... This book is wonderful in it's troubling exasperating Jack and Isis going in circles-ness. If only something magical would happen... you know? Like maybe a Fairy Godmother would show up

This book put me through the ringer. The emotions, feels, anger and love I have for Morgan and Drew Kelley is just all too real. I feel this could be a couple friend of mine and they are just laying their ups and downs out there for me to understand and sympathize with. And boy! With the story Ms. Woodruff told, these two needed the love and sympathy from friends and family more now than they ever did.

"An Underestimated Christmas" Jettie Woodruff. Picks up a few years into the future with their son 5 year old son Nicholas and 3 year old son Weston (nickname Tadpole). Like all couples with kids there seems to be a separation building between Morgan and Drew on how to raise their kids. Drew feels his son Nicholas is beyond exceptional and can do no wrong. But Morgan feels there is something underlying with Nicholas' behavior that is causing him to out more than normal. When Morgan's assumption becomes reality. How will they cope to get Nicholas through the toughest hurdle of his life. Will they come together as one unit or will denial and a destructive downward spiral cause them to lose it all?

Everything Changes is a book that made me take a step back and think hard about it. I wasn't sure how I was going to rate, so many things worked for me and then again so many things didn't. I rode the fence on this one. I chatted with Kim and we pondered for a while, bouncing thoughts off each other. Both pointing out different aspects of the story and defending why we felt the way we did. To me this is what reading is about. Everything Changes made me feel, made me remember why I am so addicted to words that run across the pages. Reading is an escape for me, takes me away from the real world. I lost my focus and it was no longer that escape, it was more like a job. Shey Stahl is amazing with her dialogue and her descriptions. My first foray into her world was fascinating, and she has made me a fan. I know Everything Changes has some mixed reviews, I have read them. Even before, I started Parker and Ro's journey. I can totally see all the different valid points and even agree with many, but for me this story just worked. top_five_star_obscene.text: I have read all of JL Perry's books and I must say this is her best book...... Don't let the title of this book deter you from reading this book, it's a beautiful story........ Carter Reynold's is born a Bastard (his mother was unmarried when he was born, hence the title of this book Bastard)he has a really tough life, actually he hates life and everything and any one.... he learnt from a very young age that he was a "Bastard" and tended to act this way.... that is until he meets the girl next door, Indiana Montgomery (Indi) he takes an instant dislike to her, why because she tends to stir up feelings in him that he doesn't want to have..... Indi has also had a pretty tough life, her mother died when she was 6 years old and she's been bought up by her father..... When Carter and his mother move next door to Indi and her father, Indi's life takes a turn for the worse... Carter is sexy and absolutely gorgeous but that's about where it ends he makes her life hell. We follow the story of Carter and Indi through lots of twists and turns that you will not see coming..... Will Carter and Indi end up together????? What other possible dramas could they both have in their lives???? This books is a stand a lone...... It was a really beautiful story.. one that I enjoyed...... I received an ARC from the Author for an Honest Review and have purchased a copy as well.
top_five_star_obscene.title: A beautiful story... don't let the Title stop you reading it......

Haven has a quick mouth that gets her into trouble and Jake/Harry (what do I call him?!?!) has a wide smile that could drop your panties in and flash. what’s not to love?!

Haven’s a little bit of a bitch, but she’s not entirely doing it on purpose. She’s incredibly focused on her job and really wants to succeed there. She knows that she’s not part of the “popular” crowd at the office and so she falls onto her defense position: be a bitch, get s*** done. Really, it’s just a cover for her deep-seated insecurities. When she gets the job to interview an music star turned actress, she nearly screams out of her skin. She’s determined to make this perfect.

But then she’s paired with Jake Harrison (thus, Harry), a photographer, who has been assigned to document the interview. Haven thinks he’s a spoiled rich boy, living off daddy’s trust fund who only got this job because he’s friends with her company’s owner. She also sort of tells him that. Oops. What she doesn’t know is that she actually only got the job because her boss thinks she’s not that pretty and he needed someone who wouldn’t outshine the actress. Crap.

I love reading Selena Kitt’s Bad Boy Boxsets. She knows how to pick the best authors for her boxset. “Bad Boy Fever” is a boxset of 10 books by 10 awesome authors that know how to write about bad boys. You have Selena Kitt, Emme Rollins, Missy Johnson, Alexis Abbott, Savannah May, Arabella Abbing, Kendra King, Kimberly Fox, JB Duvane, and Liz Adams. This is the very first book I’ve read of Ms. Curtis’, and I can honestly say it will not be the last. I was a little skeptical going into it because I’d never really been into small town girl romances, but I wanted to give it a try because I know Laura is a hoot, and I knew her books would at least entertain me. Also, she gave it to me for free, so there is that, lol.

When I first read the book blurb, I was intrigued – sex toys in small town Tennessee? It was either going to be epically, catastrophically terrible or it would be a riot of humor, sensuality, surprises, and a bit of true love. I was so pleased to discover it was the latter.

I gave Toying with His Affections a score of 5 out of 5, which I do not give out lightly. Here are my reasons.

I appreciated that the heroine, Evie, was strong and independent without being inconsiderate, selfish, self-centered, and an all-around bitch. I HATE writers who think strong and independent are synonymous with bitchy and harpy. If you’ve read my other reviews, you’ll see the pattern of dislike mentioned in several posts. Evie, a self-made woman, left Fairview years before to make her own mark on the world, but when her aunt (the woman who raised her after her mother died when she was 8yo) hurts herself and needs help, Evie drops everything and makes the drive from Las Vegas to Fairview -- small town Am
top_five_star_obscene.title: Juicy Review -- Toying with His Affections by Laura K. Curtis

top_five_star_obscene.text:
This series just keeps getting better and better! Once again, author Jus Accardo has done it again. She grabbed my attention and kept a hold of it until the very end! There were so many shock worthy moments that left me reeling.

The story picks up a few months after the end of book 2. Kale has given himself to Dez's father to save Dez's life. In true Marshal Cross style, he only wanted Kale, and couldn't care less if Dez was cured of the poison that was coursing through her body. Not a day has gone by where Kale wasn't the first thing on Dez's mind, and life without him is pretty unbearable. All she wants to do is go and find Kale and bring him back home before the people at Denazen (aka her after) does something horrible to him. But time is ticking for the Supremacy kids and the Underground are determined to find as many as they can, before they die whether it be due to the drug that caused all this, or by the hands of Denazen.

Even though going to a party is the last thing that Dez wants to do, but it's at one of these parties that Dez has one of the most shocking moments of her life. Not only does she run into her crazy ass sister, Kiernan, but it seems that Kale is now her boy toy, and ready to kill Dez.
